ticket.title: Deletion propagation into the KB / writeback / vectorized artifacts
ticket.description: GDPR right-to-deletion must cascade beyond DynamoDB+S3 (a linked ticket) into KB writeback and vectorized artifacts. Trace writeback provenance (source_user_id, s3_key) in a deletion-eligible index; on DELETE /api/user/account, remove the user's derived KB content. Security/compliance, not optional.

candidate_memory.link_reason: the deletion obligation this ticket implements
the writeback the deletion must reverse

code_agent.rationale: code_agent keeps the in-scope codebase/decision context; agent_run/release/workspace is out of scope, local notes are private.

qa_agent.rationale: qa_agent keeps the in-scope codebase/decision context; agent_run/workspace is out of scope, local notes are private; 'a linked ticket Executable Launch Plan' is in scope but tangential here (optional).

design_agent.rationale: design_agent keeps the in-scope decision/workspace context; agent_run/codebase/release is out of scope, local notes are private; 'Decision #15: user memory in DynamoDB' is in scope but tangential here (optional).

product_agent.rationale: product_agent keeps the in-scope decision/workspace context; agent_run/codebase is out of scope, local notes are private; 'Decision #15: user memory in DynamoDB', 'a linked ticket Executable Launch Plan' are in scope but tangential here (optional).

human_teammate.rationale: Robin (the baseline owner) sees the current team entries; another actor's local note stays on its owner's device.
